Interface ISudokuSolver

All Known Implementing Classes:
SudokuSolver
public interface ISudokuSolver
Provides operations for solving a Sudoku grid and for validating cell placements and grid states.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    int
    countSolutions()
    Calculates the number of solutions that a sudoku puzzle currently has.
    boolean
    hasUniqueSolution()
    Determines whether or not the sudoku puzzle has exactly one valid solution.
    static boolean
    isGridStateValid(sudoku.ISudokuState grid_to_check)
    Checks to ensure the state of the grid provided is legal in terms of the Sudoku game rules.
    static boolean
    isPlacementValid(sudoku.ISudokuState grid_to_check, int row, int column, int value)
    Check whether a move is legal in a given grid state.
    boolean
    isSolvable()
    Uses backtracking to compute whether or not the grid in its current state is solvable.
    boolean
    isValidMove(int row, int column, int value)
    Checks whether setting a given cell to a certain value would follow the rules of Sudoku.
    Set<ISudokuGrid>
    solveGrid()
    Computes a set of solutions for the sudoku grid in its current state.
    Set<ISudokuGrid>
    solveGrid(int solutions_required)
    Computes a set of solutions for the sudoku grid in its current state.

  • Method Details

    • isSolvable

      boolean isSolvable()
      Uses backtracking to compute whether or not the grid in its current state is solvable.
      Returns:
      whether or not the sudoku puzzle is solvable

    • hasUniqueSolution

      boolean hasUniqueSolution()
      Determines whether or not the sudoku puzzle has exactly one valid solution.
      Returns:
      true if the puzzle has one solution; false otherwise

    • countSolutions

      int countSolutions()
      Calculates the number of solutions that a sudoku puzzle currently has.

      Note that this method is only safe to call on grids which are near-complete, as calling on a grid with many possible solutions may result in an OutOfMemoryError.

      Returns:
      the number of possible solutions of the grid in its current state.

    • solveGrid

      Set<ISudokuGrid> solveGrid()
      Computes a set of solutions for the sudoku grid in its current state.
      Returns:
      the (possibly empty) set of all possible solutions for the grid.

    • solveGrid

      Set<ISudokuGrid> solveGrid(int solutions_required)
      Computes a set of solutions for the sudoku grid in its current state.
      Parameters:
      solutions_required - The maximum number of solutions that should be generated.
      Returns:
      the (possibly empty) set of possible solutions for the grid.

    • isValidMove

      boolean isValidMove(int row, int column, int value)
      Checks whether setting a given cell to a certain value would follow the rules of Sudoku.

      Coordinates are indexed from (0, 0), which is the upper left-most cell in the grid.

      Parameters:
      row - the row (y-coordinate) of the cell to check.
      column - the column (x-coordinate) of the cell to check.
      value - the value to test.
      Returns:
      whether the value can be legally placed in the cell

    • isPlacementValid

      static boolean isPlacementValid(sudoku.ISudokuState grid_to_check, int row, int column, int value)
      Check whether a move is legal in a given grid state.

      Coordinates are indexed from (0, 0), which is the upper left-most cell in the grid.

      Note that this function does not check whether the current grid state itself is valid, only whether or not the placement of a specific cell is.

      Parameters:
      grid_to_check - The current state of the grid to check.
      row - The row (y-coordinate) of the cell to check.
      column - The column (x-coordinate) of the cell to check
      value - The value to be checked.
      Returns:
      Whether value can be inserted into grid_to_check at (row, column).

    • isGridStateValid

      static boolean isGridStateValid(sudoku.ISudokuState grid_to_check)
      Checks to ensure the state of the grid provided is legal in terms of the Sudoku game rules.
      Parameters:
      grid_to_check - The grid state to check.
      Returns:
      Whether the grid state is valid.
